p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, stable orientation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remains consistent throughout the sequence; no significant morphological shifts observed.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, two primary leaves, one secondary sprout.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The leaf margins show persistent dehydration patterns consistent with the T-4 baseline.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Persistent marginal necrosis suggests ongoing VPD/transpiration imbalance.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit scale anchor (5cm).
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf posture remains static relative to the rabbit anchor; no new growth or senescence detected.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. Minimal tip necrosis noted previously remains unchanged.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the p2/p4 shared pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: No measurable change in size or leaf orientation over the 5-day period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Dormant/Stagnant. Growth rate is currently negligible.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Consistent presence of white particulate matter (perlite/top-dressing) across all pots.
biome_observations.desk_surface: No debris or external contamination detected.

temporal_deltas.summary: The biome has entered a state of stasis. No significant physiological progression or regression observed between T-4 and CURRENT.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a 'maintenance' phase. While p2 remains stressed, there is no acute decline. The lack of change suggests the current environmental variables are insufficient to trigger growth but stable enough to prevent further degradation.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil is confirmed as a successful user-applied top-dressing/amendment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a stable but stagnant biome. p1 and p3 maintain their baseline health. p2 continues to exhibit signs of chronic stress, likely due to the previously identified sensor failure and environmental VPD. No new growth or negative developments were detected across the sequence.
